questions.text: I rarely wake up from a dream still gripped by its emotional content.
I find that I rarely need coping strategies because my emotional system self-regulates efficiently.
I find that tracking my personal milestones keeps me motivated and on course.
I frequently volunteer to be the spokesperson or point person.
I notice that I instinctively ask others which tasks they feel best suited for.
I tend not to notice when someone deliberately includes me in a conversation by directing a question my way.
I believe that clear-headed reasoning during emotional situations is a contribution that is both valuable and underrecognized.
I feel a strong pull to be deeply involved in important initiatives, though not necessarily in charge.
I detect the emotional residue in a room where something intense just happened.
I respond when I can and trust that people will follow up if something is truly urgent.
I schedule regular maintenance for my car, home, and other possessions.
I think that treating distressing events as occasions for analytical problem-solving is the posture that maximizes my ability to contribute something of lasting value.
I find satisfaction in helping someone else's vision come to life.
I believe that the moral priority of addressing suffering is a truth that should inform the policies, institutions, and norms of any decent society.
I believe personal gain means little if the people around me are not better off.
I find that the projects I initiate for myself are the ones I pour the most energy into.
I find that I'm living life with the amplifier turned to ten.
I hold the view that the weight of my moral commitments is tested most honestly in moments when I encounter suffering I did not expect.
I prefer clear instructions over subtle hints about what is expected.
I am drawn to being the person who makes the toughest calls.
I rarely make backup plans because I trust my ability to improvise if things go sideways.
I feel a deep sense of responsibility for the safety and wellbeing of those around me.
I do my best work under time pressure with minimal preparation.
I plan backward from deadlines, identifying each milestone that must be hit along the way.
I find that my analytical approach to emotionally difficult situations allows me to craft responses that are proportionate, targeted, and effective.
I tend to undercharge or waive fees when someone genuinely cannot afford what I offer.
I experience loyalty and attachment with a fierceness that can be difficult to regulate.
I notice when someone breaks eye contact at a telling moment.
I usually do not notice when someone is hinting at something rather than stating it outright.
I find that my emotional responses are measured and proportional to the situation.
I feel that a structured approach to problem-solving leads to more reliable outcomes.
I believe that my confidence grows when I consistently invest in myself.
I notice when a person's vocal cadence shifts to match the person they are trying to connect with.
I believe that reducing my own consumption so others can have more is a worthy practice.
I tend to be the first person in a room to react emotionally to what's happening.
I think of myself as someone who drives outcomes.
